sanskrit:
वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम्।
कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम्॥
transliteration:
vedāvināśinaṃ nityaṃ ya enam ajam avyayam
kathaṃ sa puruṣaḥ pārtha kaṃ ghātayati hanti kam
literal_meaning: One who knows this soul as indestructible, eternal, unborn, immutable — how, O Pārtha, can that person cause to slay or slay whom?
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes Arjuna’s moral paralysis by asserting that a mind anchored in the eternal Self cannot be a perpetrator or victim of real harm; the fear of ‘killing’ dissolves when one sees the unbroken continuity of consciousness.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance: the ego clings to ‘do-no-harm’ while the deeper Self knows no death, creating an existential vertigo.
trigger_scenario: A surgeon hesitates to operate on a loved one, fearing pain, until a mentor reminds: ‘You’re not cutting the life, only the illness.’
anchor_text: See the eternal Self and act; no sword can touch what never dies.
